what_it_does: Configures TypeScript for the surface/app package: it sets compiler options for a modern browser/React project and limits compilation to files under the src directory.
observations: Targets ES2020 with DOM and DOM.Iterable libraries
Uses ESNext modules with bundler module resolution
Enables allowImportingTsExtensions and noEmit, so TypeScript is used for type-checking only
Strict mode is enabled, but noUnusedLocals is explicitly false
include is set to src only
standouts: moduleResolution: bundler paired with allowImportingTsExtensions and noEmit strongly suggests a Vite-like bundler workflow
Strict mode combined with noUnusedLocals: false is a deliberate relaxation
conventions: Follows modern TypeScript React app configuration conventions
Separates source from other files via include
concerns: noUnusedLocals: false can hide dead local variables
include only covers src, so tests or config files outside src may not be type-checked
No explicit outDir or declaration settings because noEmit is true
notes: A tight, modern tsconfig for a React app that lets the bundler handle emit while keeping most strict checks on.
questions: Which bundler/build tool consumes this configuration?
Is noUnusedLocals disabled intentionally to reduce noise during development?
Are tests intended to be type-checked under a separate tsconfig?
intention_signals: It appears designed to type-check a React application compiled by a modern bundler, allowing direct .ts/.tsx imports without emitting compiled files.
contribution: Establishes the TypeScript compiler contract for the app package, including target environment, module system, JSX handling, strictness, and the source scope.
summary_for_embedding: company/surface/app/tsconfig.json is a strict, noEmit TypeScript configuration for a React app using ESNext modules, bundler resolution, and the react-jsx transform over src.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to configure TypeScript type-checking for a modern bundler-based React application while relaxing unused-local enforcement.
